mechanism_narrative: INDPRO is a proxy for industrial output, and a sustained upward trend indicates expanding production capacity and economic strength, which historically leads to positive equity returns. This signal is always active; no trigger condition is required.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is consistently negative over any 3-year window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor market weakness, which reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns. The signal activates when UNRATE exceeds a moderate threshold.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is not negative with 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong GDP growth and accommodative monetary policy create a favorable macro backdrop for equities: high demand signals robust earnings, while low interest rates compress discount rates. The joint condition is required for the signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized QoQ growth > 2% and FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Rank IC is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint condition does not produce positive returns in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises above a moderate pace while the labor market is already tight (low unemployment), markets anticipate monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations. The signal only activates in a specific low-unemployment regime.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC in this regime is not negative over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer spending, which is a primary driver of GDP and corporate revenues. When RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds a high threshold, it signals above-trend demand and positive near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Average 5-day return following trigger is not positive, or the trigger fails to outperform a simple long S&P 500 position in a holdout period.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate signals restrictive monetary policy that increases the cost of capital and depresses corporate valuations. The signal activates when FEDFUNDS exceeds a level historically associated with tightening cycles.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when trigger is active (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC is not negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A strengthening labor market, evidenced by falling unemployment and rising nonfarm payrolls simultaneously, points to broad economic expansion and supports corporate earnings growth. The joint movement of both indicators amplifies the signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreasing month-over-month and PAYEMS increasing month-over-month
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal does not improve upon the predictive power of either series individually.

mechanism_narrative: In a recession regime (falling GDP), positive payroll growth would be a contrarian indicator of economic resilience and early recovery, leading to positive forward returns. The signal only applies when the macro regime is contractionary.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are not significantly positive in recessionary periods when payrolls rise, or the signal fails to provide a positive Rank IC in such regimes over any historical recession.
